message:
feat(verification): add claim/evidence graph contract (AA-10.1)

4-file contract bead for AA-10.1: graph schema with 7 node types
(CLAIM, EVIDENCE, POLICY, TRACE, WORKLOAD, TEST, ROLLBACK) and 7 edge
types (SUPPORTS, REFUTES, GOVERNS, PRODUCES, REPLAYS, OBSERVES,
TRIGGERS). Bundle contract with 6 validation rules. 33-test invariant
suite covering schema structure, edge endpoint validity, claim lifecycle,
policy coverage, rollback commands, and full bundle roundtrip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.1","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-10.1] Define claim/evidence/policy/trace graph schema and artifact bundle contract","description":"Define the graph schema and bundle contract that connect runtime claims to evidence, policies, traces, workloads, tests, and rollback triggers across the entire ascension program.","design":"Scope:\n- Standardize IDs and linkage rules for claims, evidence, policies, traces, workloads, and decisions.\n- Keep the schema simple enough for humans to inspect and tooling to validate.\n- Make it the default closure language for the full program.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The schema covers claim, evidence, policy, trace, workload, and rollback linkage.\n2. Bundles can be validated deterministically and are versioned.\n3. Unit tests protect schema evolution and bundle validation.\n4. Other ascension tracks can plug their artifacts into the schema without ad hoc mapping.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.10.4 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.10.4.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:15.124321763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:00:01.532872893Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T06:00:01.532752740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","formal","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:15.124321763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.2","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-10.2] Implement CI gates for proof artifacts, calibration drift, and tail regressions","description":"Implement the hard gates that make the ascension program operationally real: proof/artifact consistency checks, calibration-drift alarms, tail-regression budgets, and rollout readiness validation.","design":"Scope:\n- Fail closed on missing or mismatched evidence.\n- Make calibration drift and tail regressions first-class CI failures.\n- Integrate with deterministic unit/E2E/bench flows and evidence bundles.","acceptance_criteria":"1. CI can validate artifact bundles, controller calibration state, and tail-budget deltas.\n2. Failures are actionable and point to the exact broken claim, artifact, or workload.\n3. Deterministic test/bench commands and structured logs are part of the gate.\n4. Progressive-delivery readiness is computed from explicit evidence, not human memory.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.10.5 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Closure packet links every important claim to evidence, workloads, and rollback triggers.\n2. Comparative demos and summaries distinguish default-ready work from experimental work.\n3. Known risks, non-goals, and next-step decisions are explicit.\n4. Final docs/logs/artifacts are self-contained enough to act as the continuation point for future sessions.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.10.6 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.10.6.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:25.090782543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:00:10.911238094Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T06:00:10.911104426Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","release","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:25.090782543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","title":"[AA-10.1] Define claim/evidence/policy/trace graph schema and artifact bundle contract","description":"Define the graph schema and bundle contract that connect runtime claims to evidence, policies, traces, workloads, tests, and rollback triggers across the entire ascension program.","design":"Scope:\n- Standardize IDs and linkage rules for claims, evidence, policies, traces, workloads, and decisions.\n- Keep the schema simple enough for humans to inspect and tooling to validate.\n- Make it the default closure language for the full program.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The schema covers claim, evidence, policy, trace, workload, test, and rollback linkage.
2. Bundles can be validated deterministically, are versioned, and have direct replay or inspection commands.
3. Deterministic unit tests and bundle round-trip E2E scripts protect schema evolution and bundle validation.
4. Other ascension tracks can plug their artifacts into the schema without ad hoc mapping, and structured validation logs explain any rejection at human- and machine-readable levels.","notes":"Primary risk: schema overgrowth.\nCountermeasure: version early and keep the core graph minimal.\nFallback: allow reduced bundles but mark the linked claims non-default or provisional.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:04.916788136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:32:13.659737052Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","formal","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:32:13.659683383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:04.916788136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:48.055629045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:50.534639470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

The schema covers claim, evidence, policy, trace, workload, test, and rollback linkage.
2. Bundles can be validated deterministically, are versioned, and have direct replay or inspection commands.
3. Deterministic unit tests and bundle round-trip E2E scripts protect schema evolution and bundle validation.
4. Other ascension tracks can plug their artifacts into the schema without ad hoc mapping, and structured validation logs explain any rejection at human- and machine-readable levels.","notes":"Primary risk: schema overgrowth.\nCountermeasure: version early and keep the core graph minimal.\nFallback: allow reduced bundles but mark the linked claims non-default or provisional.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:04.916788136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:21:47.342446170Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T20:21:47.342422797Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","formal","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:32:13.659683383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:04.916788136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:48.055629045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:50.534639470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","title":"[AA-10.2] Implement CI gates for proof artifacts, calibration drift, and tail regressions","description":"Implement the hard gates that make the ascension program operationally real: proof/artifact consistency checks, calibration-drift alarms, tail-regression budgets, and rollout readiness validation.","design":"Scope:\n- Fail closed on missing or mismatched evidence.\n- Make calibration drift and tail regressions first-class CI failures.\n- Integrate with deterministic unit, E2E, and bench flows plus evidence bundles.","acceptance_criteria":"1. CI can validate artifact bundles, controller calibration state, track validation-pack coverage, composition eligibility, and tail-budget deltas.
2. Failures are actionable and point to the exact broken claim, artifact, workload, missing validation pack, or unsupported combination.
3. Deterministic unit, E2E, and bench commands plus structured logs are part of the gate, and failures emit exact rerun commands.
4. Progressive-delivery readiness is computed from explicit evidence, per-track coverage status, and composition status, not human memory.","notes":"Primary risk: noisy gates that people learn to ignore.\nCountermeasure: tie each gate to a concrete claim or invariant and tune against the AA-01 corpus.\nFallback: degraded or experimental tracks stay out of default rollout if evidence is insufficient.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:10.193652802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:42.878289387Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","ci","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:10.193652802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:53.033214189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:32:16.167470743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:55.533223182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:58.056030570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:00.574227580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:03.076018953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","title":"[AA-10.3] Produce comparative demos, closure packet, and launch doctrine for runtime ascension","description":"Produce the final closure packet for the ascension program: comparative demos, claim-backed benchmark summaries, known-risk register, rollback doctrine, and the operator/developer guidance needed to continue or ship the work responsibly.","design":"Scope:\n- Summarize what actually improved, what remained experimental, and why.\n- Include side-by-side comparisons against conservative baselines and any valid external references.\n- Leave behind a package future contributors can extend without reconstructing the original intent.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Closure packet links every important claim to evidence, unit tests, workloads, structured logs, rollback triggers, and cross-track compatibility verdicts.
2. Comparative demos and deterministic E2E drill scripts distinguish default-ready work from experimental work and explain the user-visible difference.
3. Known risks, non-goals, downgrade paths, unsupported combinations, and next-step decisions are explicit.
4. Final docs, runnable verification commands, composition-drill recipes, structured incident logs, and operator/developer troubleshooting recipes are self-contained enough to act as the continuation and incident-response point for future sessions.","notes":"Primary risk: turning closure into marketing instead of truth.\nCountermeasure: every headline result must cite the underlying evidence graph.\nFallback: if a track lacks closure-quality evidence, it remains explicitly experimental in the launch doctrine.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:15.209441938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:18:25.452896555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","release","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:15.209441938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:05.571178582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:37.874942303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:08.062936829Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:10.619028203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:13.114106178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","title":"[AA-10.4] Run cross-track composition gauntlet, compatibility matrix, and incident drill pack","description":"Execute the full-system validation layer that the per-track beads do not cover on their own: deterministic composition tests across interacting advanced features, a compatibility matrix, and operator-grade incident drill artifacts.","design":"Scope:
- Compose the graduating tracks in the combinations that matter to real users: decision plane, control artifacts, fast-path substrate, static safety surfaces, trace intelligence, authority sandboxing, crash-only recovery, and transport experiments.
- Treat compatibility as a first-class artifact rather than an assumption hidden inside other validation beads.
- Build deterministic drills for incident handling, downgrade, rollback, and replay across track boundaries.
- Produce a matrix that distinguishes supported default combinations, supported-but-experimental combinations, and forbidden combinations.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E composition suites cover high-risk combinations across controllers, fast paths, static surfaces, trace intelligence, authority, recovery, and transport.
2. A compatibility matrix names supported, degraded, experimental, and forbidden combinations together with explicit fallback or downgrade behavior.
3. Structured logs, incident packets, and exact rerun commands let an operator reproduce any failing composition scenario directly.
4. Any cross-track regression in invariants, tail budgets, recovery behavior, authority containment, or diagnosability blocks closure.","notes":"Why this bead exists:
- Per-track validation is necessary but not sufficient. Users experience the composed system, not isolated tracks.
- The biggest late-stage risk in an ambitious runtime program is interaction failure: features that are individually correct but jointly unstable, opaque, or operationally painful.
- This bead is the plan-space answer to that failure mode.

Fallback doctrine:
- If a combination is not proven, it is explicitly marked unsupported or experimental.
- Default-ready closure requires both per-track green status and composition-level green status.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:10.178075830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:55.194329811Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","ci","release","runtime-ascension","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:10.178075830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:15.179026009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:17.692032263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:20.216484275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:22.736266190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:25.237995174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:27.734485164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:55.194257877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:30.257797392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:32.763947553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:35.339999483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,170 @@
+{
+  "contract_version": "claim-evidence-graph-v1",
+  "bead_id": "asupersync-1508v.10.4",
+  "description": "AA-10.1 claim/evidence/policy/trace graph schema and artifact bundle contract for runtime ascension closure.",
+  "runner_script": "scripts/run_claim_evidence_graph_smoke.sh",
+  "runner_bundle_schema_version": "claim-evidence-graph-smoke-bundle-v1",
+  "runner_report_schema_version": "claim-evidence-graph-smoke-run-report-v1",
+  "graph_schema": {
+    "description": "Minimal graph linking runtime claims to their supporting evidence, governing policies, and replay traces.",
+    "node_types": [
+      {
+        "type_id": "CLAIM",
+        "description": "A verifiable assertion about runtime behavior (e.g., 'p99 scheduling latency <= 500us').",
+        "required_fields": ["claim_id", "description", "category", "status"],
+        "status_values": ["asserted", "evidenced", "verified", "revoked"],
+        "category_values": ["safety", "performance", "correctness", "fairness"]
+      },
+      {
+        "type_id": "EVIDENCE",
+        "description": "An artifact or measurement that supports or refutes a claim.",
+        "required_fields": ["evidence_id", "kind", "source_artifact", "timestamp"],
+        "kind_values": ["test_result", "benchmark", "proof_artifact", "calibration_score", "replay_trace", "audit_finding"]
+      },
+      {
+        "type_id": "POLICY",
+        "description": "A rule governing claim evaluation, promotion, or rejection.",
+        "required_fields": ["policy_id", "description", "enforcement"],
+        "enforcement_values": ["mandatory", "advisory", "informational"]
+      },
+      {
+        "type_id": "TRACE",
+        "description": "A replayable execution trace linked to a workload.",
+        "required_fields": ["trace_id", "workload_id", "snapshot_range", "duration_us"]
+      },
+      {
+        "type_id": "WORKLOAD",
+        "description": "A reproducible workload from the replay corpus.",
+        "required_fields": ["workload_id", "description", "profile"]
+      },
+      {
+        "type_id": "TEST",
+        "description": "A deterministic test that produces evidence.",
+        "required_fields": ["test_id", "test_file", "test_name", "kind"],
+        "kind_values": ["unit", "integration", "e2e", "smoke", "compile_fail"]
+      },
+      {
+        "type_id": "ROLLBACK",
+        "description": "A rollback trigger linked to a claim violation.",
+        "required_fields": ["rollback_id", "claim_id", "reason", "command"]
+      }
+    ],
+    "edge_types": [
+      {
+        "edge_id": "SUPPORTS",
+        "from": "EVIDENCE",
+        "to": "CLAIM",
+        "description": "Evidence supports a claim."
+      },
+      {
+        "edge_id": "REFUTES",
+        "from": "EVIDENCE",
+        "to": "CLAIM",
+        "description": "Evidence refutes a claim."
+      },
+      {
+        "edge_id": "GOVERNS",
+        "from": "POLICY",
+        "to": "CLAIM",
+        "description": "Policy governs claim evaluation."
+      },
+      {
+        "edge_id": "PRODUCES",
+        "from": "TEST",
+        "to": "EVIDENCE",
+        "description": "Test produces evidence."
+      },
+      {
+        "edge_id": "REPLAYS",
+        "from": "TRACE",
+        "to": "WORKLOAD",
+        "description": "Trace replays a workload."
+      },
+      {
+        "edge_id": "OBSERVES",
+        "from": "TRACE",
+        "to": "EVIDENCE",
+        "description": "Trace produces observational evidence."
+      },
+      {
+        "edge_id": "TRIGGERS",
+        "from": "CLAIM",
+        "to": "ROLLBACK",
+        "description": "Claim violation triggers rollback."
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "bundle_contract": {
+    "description": "An artifact bundle packages claims, evidence, policies, and traces for validation.",
+    "bundle_schema_version": "claim-evidence-bundle-v1",
+    "required_sections": ["claims", "evidence", "policies", "edges"],
+    "optional_sections": ["traces", "workloads", "tests", "rollbacks"],
+    "validation_rules": [
+      {
+        "rule_id": "V-CLAIM-EVIDENCE",
+        "description": "Every evidenced or verified claim must have at least one SUPPORTS edge.",
+        "severity": "error"
+      },
+      {
+        "rule_id": "V-EDGE-REFS",
+        "description": "All edge endpoints must reference existing nodes.",
+        "severity": "error"
+      },
+      {
+        "rule_id": "V-POLICY-COVERAGE",
+        "description": "Every safety claim must be governed by at least one mandatory policy.",
+        "severity": "error"
+      },
+      {
+        "rule_id": "V-ROLLBACK-COMMAND",
+        "description": "Every rollback must have a non-empty command.",
+        "severity": "error"
+      },
+      {
+        "rule_id": "V-TEST-EVIDENCE",
+        "description": "Evidence of kind test_result should have a PRODUCES edge from a TEST.",
+        "severity": "warning"
+      },
+      {
+        "rule_id": "V-TRACE-WORKLOAD",
+        "description": "Every trace must REPLAYS exactly one workload.",
+        "severity": "warning"
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "structured_log_fields_required": [
+    "bundle_id",
+    "bundle_version",
+    "validation_rule_id",
+    "validation_severity",
+    "validation_pass",
+    "validation_message",
+    "claim_id",
+    "claim_status",
+    "evidence_count",
+    "policy_count",
+    "edge_count",
+    "node_type",
+    "graph_connected"
+  ],
+  "smoke_scenarios": [
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "CEG-SMOKE-SCHEMA",
+      "description": "Validate graph schema node and edge type definitions",
+      "command": "rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa101 cargo test --test claim_evidence_graph_contract schema -- --nocapture"
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "CEG-SMOKE-BUNDLE",
+      "description": "Validate bundle contract and validation rules",
+      "command": "rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa101 cargo test --test claim_evidence_graph_contract bundle -- --nocapture"
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "CEG-SMOKE-VALIDATION",
+      "description": "Validate graph validation logic and rejection reporting",
+      "command": "rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa101 cargo test --test claim_evidence_graph_contract validation -- --nocapture"
+    }
+  ],
+  "downstream_beads": [
+    "asupersync-1508v.10.5"
+  ]
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,105 @@
+# Claim/Evidence Graph Contract
+
+Bead: `asupersync-1508v.10.4`
+
+## Purpose
+
+This contract defines the graph schema that connects runtime claims to their supporting evidence, governing policies, replay traces, workloads, tests, and rollback triggers. It is the default closure language for the entire ascension program.
+
+## Contract Artifacts
+
+1. Canonical artifact: `artifacts/claim_evidence_graph_v1.json`
+2. Smoke runner: `scripts/run_claim_evidence_graph_smoke.sh`
+3. Invariant suite: `tests/claim_evidence_graph_contract.rs`
+
+## Graph Schema
+
+### Node Types
+
+| Type | Description | Key Fields |
+|------|-------------|------------|
+| CLAIM | Verifiable runtime assertion | claim_id, category, status |
+| EVIDENCE | Supporting/refuting artifact | evidence_id, kind, source_artifact |
+| POLICY | Evaluation/promotion rule | policy_id, enforcement |
+| TRACE | Replayable execution trace | trace_id, workload_id |
+| WORKLOAD | Reproducible workload | workload_id, profile |
+| TEST | Deterministic test | test_id, test_file, kind |
+| ROLLBACK | Claim-violation trigger | rollback_id, claim_id, command |
+
+### Edge Types
+
+| Edge | From | To | Meaning |
+|------|------|----|---------|
+| SUPPORTS | EVIDENCE | CLAIM | Evidence supports claim |
+| REFUTES | EVIDENCE | CLAIM | Evidence refutes claim |
+| GOVERNS | POLICY | CLAIM | Policy governs claim evaluation |
+| PRODUCES | TEST | EVIDENCE | Test produces evidence |
+| REPLAYS | TRACE | WORKLOAD | Trace replays workload |
+| OBSERVES | TRACE | EVIDENCE | Trace produces observations |
+| TRIGGERS | CLAIM | ROLLBACK | Claim violation triggers rollback |
+
+### Claim Lifecycle
+
+Claims progress through: `asserted` -> `evidenced` -> `verified` -> (optionally `revoked`).
+
+- `asserted`: Claim stated but no evidence linked
+- `evidenced`: At least one SUPPORTS edge exists
+- `verified`: Mandatory policies satisfied and evidence validated
+- `revoked`: Claim invalidated by REFUTES evidence or policy change
+
+## Bundle Contract
+
+An artifact bundle packages claims, evidence, policies, and edges into a single validatable unit.
+
+Required sections: `claims`, `evidence`, `policies`, `edges`
+Optional sections: `traces`, `workloads`, `tests`, `rollbacks`
+
+### Validation Rules
+
+| Rule | Severity | Description |
+|------|----------|-------------|
+| V-CLAIM-EVIDENCE | error | Evidenced/verified claims need SUPPORTS edges |
+| V-EDGE-REFS | error | All edge endpoints reference existing nodes |
+| V-POLICY-COVERAGE | error | Safety claims governed by mandatory policy |
+| V-ROLLBACK-COMMAND | error | Rollbacks have non-empty commands |
+| V-TEST-EVIDENCE | warning | test_result evidence has PRODUCES edge |
+| V-TRACE-WORKLOAD | warning | Traces REPLAYS exactly one workload |
+
+## Structured Logging Contract
+
+Validation logs MUST include:
+
+- `bundle_id`, `bundle_version`: Bundle identity
+- `validation_rule_id`, `validation_severity`, `validation_pass`, `validation_message`: Per-rule results
+- `claim_id`, `claim_status`: Claim context
+- `evidence_count`, `policy_count`, `edge_count`: Graph statistics
+- `node_type`: Node type being validated
+- `graph_connected`: Whether the graph is connected
+
+## Comparator-Smoke Runner
+
+Canonical runner: `scripts/run_claim_evidence_graph_smoke.sh`
+
+Examples:
+
+```bash
+bash ./scripts/run_claim_evidence_graph_smoke.sh --list
+bash ./scripts/run_claim_evidence_graph_smoke.sh --scenario CEG-SMOKE-SCHEMA --dry-run
+bash ./scripts/run_claim_evidence_graph_smoke.sh --execute
+```
+
+## Validation
+
+```bash
+rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa101 cargo test --test claim_evidence_graph_contract -- --nocapture
+```
+
+## Cross-References
+
+- `artifacts/claim_evidence_graph_v1.json`
+- `scripts/run_claim_evidence_graph_smoke.sh`
+- `tests/claim_evidence_graph_contract.rs`
+- `src/runtime/kernel.rs` -- ControllerRegistry, evidence ledger
+- `artifacts/runtime_control_seam_inventory_v1.json` -- Seam IDs
+- `artifacts/runtime_workload_corpus_v1.json` -- Replay workloads
+- `artifacts/decision_plane_validation_v1.json` -- Decision plane

@@ -0,0 +1,136 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Schema anchors for contract invariants:
+# - claim-evidence-graph-smoke-bundle-v1
+# - claim-evidence-graph-smoke-run-report-v1
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
+CONTRACT_ARTIFACT="${PROJECT_ROOT}/artifacts/claim_evidence_graph_v1.json"
+OUTPUT_ROOT="${CEG_SMOKE_OUTPUT_DIR:-${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/claim-evidence-graph-smoke}"
+TIMESTAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
+RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"
+LIST_ONLY=0
+DRY_RUN=1
+
+declare -a SELECTED_SCENARIOS=()
+
+usage() {
+    cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage: ./scripts/run_claim_evidence_graph_smoke.sh [options]
+
+Options:
+  --list                    List scenario IDs and exit
+  --scenario <id>           Run one scenario (repeatable)
+  --output-root <dir>       Override output root
+  --dry-run                 Emit manifests without executing (default)
+  --execute                 Execute cargo test scenarios
+  -h, --help                Show help
+USAGE
+}
+
+require_tools() {
+    if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        echo "FATAL: jq is required" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+    if [ ! -f "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" ]; then
+        echo "FATAL: contract artifact missing at ${CONTRACT_ARTIFACT}" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+}
+
+json_escape() { printf '%s' "$1" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g'; }
+contract_version() { jq -r '.contract_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+bundle_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_bundle_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+report_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_report_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+
+list_scenarios() {
+    jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[] | [.scenario_id, .description] | @tsv' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" \
+        | while IFS=$'\t' read -r sid desc; do printf '%-38s %s\n' "$sid" "$desc"; done
+}
+
+load_scenario_json() {
+    jq -c --arg sid "$1" '.smoke_scenarios[] | select(.scenario_id == $sid)' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"
+}
+
+RESULTS_JSON=""
+append_result() {
+    if [[ -z "$RESULTS_JSON" ]]; then RESULTS_JSON="$1"; else RESULTS_JSON="${RESULTS_JSON},$1"; fi
+}
+
+run_scenario() {
+    local sid="$1" scenario_json description command scenario_dir log_file summary_file started_ts ended_ts status rc
+    scenario_json="$(load_scenario_json "$sid")"
+    [[ -z "$scenario_json" ]] && { echo "FATAL: unknown scenario: $sid" >&2; return 1; }
+    description="$(jq -r '.description' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    command="$(jq -r '.command' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    scenario_dir="${RUN_DIR}/${sid}"; log_file="${scenario_dir}/run.log"; summary_file="${scenario_dir}/bundle_manifest.json"
+    mkdir -p "$scenario_dir"
+    started_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    echo ">>> Running scenario ${sid}"
+    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
+        printf 'DRY_RUN scenario=%s\n' "$sid" >"$log_file"; rc=0; status="dry_run"
+    else
+        rc=0; eval "$command" >"$log_file" 2>&1 || rc=$?
+        status="$( [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]] && printf "passed" || printf "failed" )"
+    fi
+    ended_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    cat >"$summary_file" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(bundle_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "scenario_id": "$(json_escape "$sid")",
+  "description": "$(json_escape "$description")",
+  "status": "$(json_escape "$status")",
+  "exit_code": ${rc},
+  "started_ts": "$(json_escape "$started_ts")",
+  "ended_ts": "$(json_escape "$ended_ts")"
+}
+JSON
+    append_result "$(jq -c '.' "$summary_file")"
+    [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --list) LIST_ONLY=1; shift ;;
+        --scenario) SELECTED_SCENARIOS+=("${2:-}"); shift 2 ;;
+        --output-root) OUTPUT_ROOT="${2:-}"; RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"; shift 2 ;;
+        --dry-run) DRY_RUN=1; shift ;;
+        --execute) DRY_RUN=0; shift ;;
+        -h|--help) usage; exit 0 ;;
+        *) echo "Unknown: $1" >&2; usage >&2; exit 1 ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+require_tools
+if [[ "$LIST_ONLY" -eq 1 ]]; then list_scenarios; exit 0; fi
+if [[ "${#SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
+    mapfile -t SELECTED_SCENARIOS < <(jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[].scenario_id' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT")
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR"; OVERALL_RC=0
+for sid in "${SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}"; do run_scenario "$sid" || OVERALL_RC=1; done
+
+RUN_REPORT="${RUN_DIR}/run_report.json"
+cat >"$RUN_REPORT" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(report_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "run_dir": "$(json_escape "$RUN_DIR")",
+  "dry_run": $( [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]] && printf 'true' || printf 'false' ),
+  "results": [${RESULTS_JSON}],
+  "status": "$([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "passed" || printf "failed")"
+}
+JSON
+
+echo ""
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "       CLAIM/EVIDENCE GRAPH SMOKE SUMMARY                          "
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "  Run dir:   ${RUN_DIR}"
+echo "  Mode:      $([ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ] && printf "DRY-RUN" || printf "EXECUTE")"
+echo "  Status:    $([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "PASSED" || printf "FAILED")"
+echo "==================================================================="
+
+exit "$OVERALL_RC"
